behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names IsZero(), ModuleOrigin fields, the .info filename derivation, json.Unmarshal, !mod.Origin().IsZero(), loadGitInfo and newGitInfo, and the exact log string — all referenced by tests.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction essentially dictates the fix: it specifies the exact expression `strings.TrimSuffix(m.GoMod, ".mod") + ".info"`, the exact check `!mod.Origin().IsZero()`, and explicit replacement of `h.Log.Errorln(...)` with `return err`.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert literal strings like `strings.TrimSuffix(m.GoMod, ".mod") + ".info"`, `!mod.Origin().IsZero()`, and `if m.Origin == nil && m.GoMod != ""` — alternative correct implementations (e.g., using filepath manipulation or different variable ordering) would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: test_module_origin_iszero_true_when_empty compiles/runs Go code exercising IsZero(), and pass-to-pass tests run go build/vet/test subprocesses.

test_not_tautological.reason: IsZero() behavior is actually exercised by running Go code; stub returning default bool would fail one of the two cases.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All four behavior changes listed in instruction.md (IsZero, nil Origin handling, IsZero check in gitinfo, return err in loadGitInfo) each have corresponding tests.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are named descriptively and grouped with comment separator for pass-to-pass CI gates.

structured_data_schema.reason: The .info JSON schema `{"Version":...,"Time":...,"Origin":{"VCS":...,"URL":...,"Hash":...}}` is documented in instruction.md.

no_typos.reason: Filenames and identifiers (modules/client.go, hugolib/gitinfo.go, ModuleOrigin, IsZero) are consistent between instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction pinpoints exact code to change: `h.Log.Errorln("Failed to read Git log:", err)` → `return err`, and gives exact expressions to include, removing localization as part of the task.
